questions.question: Why does mutating a property inside a `defineModel` object (e.g., `model.value.name = 'x'`) not notify the parent?
options: Vue does not allow writing to props that originate from `defineModel`
Mutating a property changes the object in place without changing the reference, so no `update:modelValue` event is emitted
Vue batches all property mutations and emits them at the end of the next tick
The parent's `v-model` binding only listens to `input` DOM events, not Vue events

questions.question: What is the correct way to update a single property inside a `defineModel` object?
options: Replace the entire object: `model.value = { ...model.value, name: newName }`
Use `reactive(model.value)` and mutate the reactive copy

questions.question: What is the issue with two consecutive spread assignments like `model.value = { ...model.value, a: '1' }` then `model.value = { ...model.value, b: '2' }`?
options: The second assignment overwrites the first because both read the same stale snapshot
It emits `update:modelValue` twice, causing unnecessary parent re-renders
Vue batches them into one update so `b` is lost
TypeScript fails to infer the merged type, causing a compile error
